title: cistus polymorphus subsp. corsicus
intro: Cistus polymorphus subsp. corsicus, also known as Corsican Rock Rose, is a plant species belonging to the Cistus genus, characterized by its pink to white flowers and silvery-gray foliage. Traditionally, the Corsican Rock Rose has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Cistus polymorphus subsp. corsicus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it accessible for various applications in traditional medicine.
